name: Currency Exchange International
description_short: Currency Exchange International specializes in foreign currency exchange, buying and selling 80+ currencies with daily inventory, plus gold bullion coins and bars at a Manhattan location.
description_long:
Currency Exchange International operates a foreign currency exchange service located on the Upper East Side of Manhattan at 801 Lexington Avenue. The company is an NYC Tourism + Conventions Member, indicating it serves both locals and tourists requiring currency services. Founded to provide an alternative to banks and airports for currency exchange, CXI has positioned itself as a convenience-focused provider in the financial services space.

The company's core offerings center on foreign currency exchange and precious metals. CXI buys and sells more than 80 foreign currencies and maintains daily inventory to serve customers on short notice. They also sell popular gold bullion coins and bars. Customers can reserve foreign currency online or by phone at (212) 421-2220 before visiting the physical branch, streamlining the transaction process. The service accepts limited EUR, GBP, CAD, and MXN coins, though no other foreign exchange coins are accepted.

CXI differentiates itself from traditional banking institutions through several advertised guarantees and service features. The company promotes a "Best Rate Guarantee" and "Currency Price Protection" to customers, emphasizing competitive pricing and security in currency transactions. Their stated mission is to provide the best exchange rates and highest level of customer service, positioning the business around the exchange experience rather than ancillary financial services.

Currency Exchange International operates as a specialized niche provider within the broader check-cashing and currency services category. While the company offers legitimate foreign exchange and precious metals services, customers should understand that independent currency exchangers typically charge spreads above interbank rates. The ability to hold inventory and provide same-day service comes at a cost reflected in their exchange rates. Customers seeking the absolute best rates on large transactions may benefit from comparing quotes, though the convenience factor and guaranteed availability of 80+ currencies represents genuine value for travelers and international remittance senders.
pros: Maintains daily inventory of 80+ foreign currencies for immediate availability, unlike banks requiring advance orders
Accepts online and phone reservations before visiting the physical branch to streamline transactions
Advertises Best Rate Guarantee and Currency Price Protection policies
Operates convenient Upper East Side location with accessible entrance on E. 62nd Street
Sells gold bullion coins and bars in addition to currency exchange services
NYC Tourism + Conventions Member status indicates institutional credibility
Accepts limited foreign currency coins (EUR, GBP, CAD, MXN) for exchange
cons: Independent currency exchangers typically apply spreads above interbank rates, making rates higher than wholesale banking options
Limited acceptance of foreign exchange coins (only EUR, GBP, CAD, MXN; no other FX coins accepted)
Single Manhattan location limits accessibility for customers outside the Upper East Side
No information provided about specific exchange rates, making rate comparison difficult before visiting
Website provides no pricing transparency, fees, or minimum transaction amounts
best_for: Travelers needing foreign currency same-day or with short notice before international trips
International business professionals requiring multiple foreign currency exchanges at one location
Customers seeking to buy or sell gold bullion coins and bars alongside currency services
NYC tourists and residents without bank accounts or those seeking alternatives to airport exchange services
diagnosis: Currency Exchange International is best for travelers, international business professionals, and NYC residents needing same-day foreign currency without bank ordering delays or wanting to buy precious metals alongside currency services. The primary caveat is that independent currency exchangers charge spreads above interbank rates, so customers should confirm their specific rates are competitive for large transactions before committing.
services: Currency inventory held in stock daily
Limited foreign currency coin exchange (EUR, GBP, CAD, MXN only)
In-person currency exchange at Manhattan branch
